schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: f3b30cb62e21c76b5779b1777ac0a6dcb98370f0
findings:
  - { id: judged-f1, kind: judged, text: "Restatement of a known, ledger-dispositioned deviation (accepted-deviation per ADJ-40, reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47), carried so this coverage commit's record stays complete: ac-3's letter says 'all three buckets are structurally present on every render of GET /', but on an index-computation failure writeGlanceSection (internal/workbench/glance.go:81-83) returns before writing anything — no \u003csection data-testid=\"home-glance\"\u003e, no bucket headings, no zero counts — so a failed-index render carries zero glance structure. The build resolves ac-3's universal quantifier against co-2's failure clause ('never renders partial or fabricated groups when the index itself failed') by omitting the whole section, a reading ratified on the workspace ledger (ADJ-40: ac-3's 'every render' is bounded by co-2) but still not declared in the spec text itself, and the failure render is pinned only by the Go unit test TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing, never by a Playwright drive of GET / under a failing index (co-1's 'every behavioral path is Playwright-proven' letter). (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Controller adjudication ADJ-40 (2026-07-16), reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47: accepted-deviation. The contract answers itself — co-2 explicitly forbids fabricated groups when the index failed (zero-count buckets on a failed index would claim knowledge the page does not have), so ac-3's 'every render' universal is bounded by co-2's failure clause. Glance-absent beside the ONE indexErr disclosure the exhaustive section already renders is the contract's own design: both sections degrade from the single shared home.Index(ctx) call, and a second glance notice would be exactly co-2's forbidden 'second, contradictory notice'. This disposition note is the store-resident record of that reading (ADJ-42 doctrine); the code comment's reasoning moves onto the ledger where it belongs. The index-failure omission is a negative-render path (pinned by the Go unit test TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing), not a behavioral defect." }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"f1\",\"text\":\"Restatement of a known, ledger-dispositioned deviation (accepted-deviation per ADJ-40, reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47), carried so this coverage commit's record stays complete: ac-3's letter says 'all three buckets are structurally present on every render of GET /', but on an index-computation failure writeGlanceSection (internal/workbench/glance.go:81-83) returns before writing anything — no \u003csection data-testid=\\\"home-glance\\\"\u003e, no bucket headings, no zero counts — so a failed-index render carries zero glance structure. The build resolves ac-3's universal quantifier against co-2's failure clause ('never renders partial or fabricated groups when the index itself failed') by omitting the whole section, a reading ratified on the workspace ledger (ADJ-40: ac-3's 'every render' is bounded by co-2) but still not declared in the spec text itself, and the failure render is pinned only by the Go unit test TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing, never by a Playwright drive of GET / under a failing index (co-1's 'every behavioral path is Playwright-proven' letter).\",\"confidence\":0.3}]}" }

---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-f1** [accepted-deviation]: Restatement of a known, ledger-dispositioned deviation (accepted-deviation per ADJ-40, reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47), carried so this coverage commit's record stays complete: ac-3's letter says 'all three buckets are structurally present on every render of GET /', but on an index-computation failure writeGlanceSection (internal/workbench/glance.go:81-83) returns before writing anything — no <section data-testid="home-glance">, no bucket headings, no zero counts — so a failed-index render carries zero glance structure. The build resolves ac-3's universal quantifier against co-2's failure clause ('never renders partial or fabricated groups when the index itself failed') by omitting the whole section, a reading ratified on the workspace ledger (ADJ-40: ac-3's 'every render' is bounded by co-2) but still not declared in the spec text itself, and the failure render is pinned only by the Go unit test TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing, never by a Playwright drive of GET / under a failing index (co-1's 'every behavioral path is Playwright-proven' letter). (confidence 0.30) — Controller adjudication ADJ-40 (2026-07-16), reconfirmed ADJ-44/ADJ-47: accepted-deviation. The contract answers itself — co-2 explicitly forbids fabricated groups when the index failed (zero-count buckets on a failed index would claim knowledge the page does not have), so ac-3's 'every render' universal is bounded by co-2's failure clause. Glance-absent beside the ONE indexErr disclosure the exhaustive section already renders is the contract's own design: both sections degrade from the single shared home.Index(ctx) call, and a second glance notice would be exactly co-2's forbidden 'second, contradictory notice'. This disposition note is the store-resident record of that reading (ADJ-42 doctrine); the code comment's reasoning moves onto the ledger where it belongs. The index-failure omission is a negative-render path (pinned by the Go unit test TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing), not a behavioral defect.
